skill.name: stockfish-analyzer
skill.description: Analyze chess positions from FEN strings using a Stockfish-backed tool. It helps explain best moves, top alternatives, and position evaluations.
skill.summary: Stockfish-backed chess position analysis from FEN input.
skill.author: CK991357

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution, weak cryptography, and high entropy indicators in SKILL.md. Manual review found false positives from Markdown backticks, fenced JSON examples, Chinese text, and chess notation. No shell execution, cryptographic logic, binary content, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

content.value_statement: Chess players need clear engine analysis without reading raw tool output. This skill turns FEN positions into best moves, alternatives, and plain-language explanations.
actual_capabilities: Detects FEN strings in user messages and selects an analysis mode.
Requests the best move for a valid chess position.
Requests multiple candidate moves when the user asks for alternatives.
Requests a position evaluation and explains centipawn or mate scores.
Converts engine-style results into beginner-friendly chess guidance.
limitations: Requires a valid FEN string for position-specific analysis.
Depends on the separate stockfish_analyzer tool being available.
Does not calculate moves itself without tool output.
Does not validate tournament legality beyond the analyzer response.

use_cases.title: Review a Game Position
use_cases.description: Analyze a saved FEN position and explain the best engine move in practical terms.

use_cases.title: Compare Candidate Moves
use_cases.description: Request several strong options and compare their strategic strengths and tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Understand Evaluation Scores
use_cases.description: Translate centipawn and mate evaluations into simple position assessments.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze This FEN
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this FEN and explain the best move in simple terms: [paste FEN]

prompt_templates.title: Find Top Moves
prompt_templates.prompt: For this FEN, show the top three moves and explain when each move is useful: [paste FEN]

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this position and explain who is better, why, and what plans matter next: [paste FEN]

prompt_templates.title: Coach a Critical Position
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Stockfish analysis on this FEN, compare the main candidates, and give a training-focused explanation: [paste FEN]

output_examples.input: User provides the starting position FEN and asks for the best move.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies a best-move request, calls the analyzer, then explains the engine move and opening purpose.

output_examples.input: User provides a middlegame FEN and asks who is better.
output_examples.output: The skill requests an evaluation, explains the score, and names the side with the practical advantage.

output_examples.input: User asks for three candidate moves from a tactical position.
output_examples.output: The skill requests multiple moves and compares their tactical and strategic ideas.

best_practices: Provide the complete FEN string before asking for analysis.
Ask for best move, top moves, or evaluation to guide the mode selection.
Use the explanation as study guidance, then verify critical lines on a board.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for analysis without sharing the current position.
Do not treat the skill as a standalone chess engine without the analyzer tool.
Do not rely on a single move explanation for formal coaching decisions.

faq.question: What input does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs a valid FEN string for the chess position you want to analyze.

faq.question: Can it show more than one move?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for top moves or candidate moves, and it will request multiple options.

faq.question: Does it make chess moves by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to use analyzer output before explaining moves.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Yes. It turns engine results into simpler explanations and study guidance.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes Chinese intent keywords for best moves, evaluations, and choices.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual audit found no confirmed dangerous behavior in the reviewed SKILL.md file.
